creator: leeym@bsd.ce.ntu.edu.tw
text:
Mmm... It's so strange.

net/icq-transport depends on the source of net/jabber, the missing file
"jabberd.h" is in ${PORTSDIR}/net/jabber/work/jabber-1.4/jabberd,
and all .c file in workdir include jabberd.h, why only mio_peer.c encounter
missing header file problem ?
I suspect that ${PORTSDIR}/net/jabber/work is removed during building
net/icq-transport .

BTW, joe@FreeBSD.org plan to merge *-transport into net/jabber. Would you
please take a look on this, joe ?

I can build net/icq-transport smoothly, and this is my make.log :
---start of make.log---
===>  Extracting for icq-transport-0.9
>> Checksum OK for icq-transport-0.9.tar.gz.
===>   icq-transport-0.9 depends on file: /nonexistent - not found
===>    Verifying configure for /nonexistent in /usr/ports/net/jabber
===>  Extracting for jabber-1.4_1
>> Checksum OK for jabber-1.4.tar.gz.
===>   jabber-1.4_1 depends on executable: pth-config - found
===>   jabber-1.4_1 depends on executable: gmake - found
===>  Patching for jabber-1.4_1
===>  Applying FreeBSD patches for jabber-1.4_1
===>  Configuring for jabber-1.4_1
Running Jabber Configure
========================

Getting pth settings...         Done.
Setting Build Parameters...     Done.
Generating Settings Script...   Done.

You may now type 'make' to build your new Jabber system.

===>   Returning to build of icq-transport-0.9
===>  Patching for icq-transport-0.9
===>  Applying FreeBSD patches for icq-transport-0.9
===>  Configuring for icq-transport-0.9
===>  Building for icq-transport-0.9
gcc -O -pipe  -g -Wall -fPIC -I. -I.. -I/usr/local/include  -I/usr/ports/net
/jabber/work/jabber-1.4/jabberd -Wall -g -c charset.c

function

/jabber/work/jabber-1.4/jabberd -Wall -g -shared -o icqtrans.so charset.o

iq.o  message.o  meta.o  mio_peer.o  mio_server.o  peer.o  presence.o
